Write a 500-word technical blog post explaining hash maps for intermediate
Python developers who know dicts but not how they work under the hood.

Format: markdown with three H2 sections (intuition, implementation, trade-offs).
Tone: conversational, no buzzwords, no "in today's world" openers.

Example style: match the style of Julia Evans' posts — specific, curious,
uses concrete numbers and concrete file paths.

If the reader's level is ambiguous, default to "has written Python, hasn't
implemented a data structure" and flag the assumption in one line.
